question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ContractCallRequest with gas set to exactly 10000 (equal to GAS_SCALE_FACTOR) so that scaleGas() returns 0, causing gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) to always succeed, thereby bypassing the gas-per-second throttle entirely and flooding the node with unlimited historical EVM simulations that replay Hashgraph state, corrupting the integrity of historical query results?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ContractCallRequest with gas set to 1 (below GAS_SCALE_FACTOR=10000) so that scaleGas() returns 0, causing gasLimitBucket to never be decremented, allowing unlimited historical block-state EVM calls that manipulate the perceived Hashgraph history without any gas throttle enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SynchronizationStrategy on rateLimitBucket (unlike gasLimitBucket which uses SYNCHRONIZED) to race concurrent HTTP requests through the rate limit check, allowing more than requestsPerSecond requests to reach historical EVM simulation, enabling mass replay of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of SynchronizationStrategy on opcodeRateLimitBucket to race concurrent requests to the /contracts/results/{transactionIdOrHash}/opcodes endpoint, bypassing the opcodeRequestsPerSecond=1 limit and replaying historical Hashgraph transactions at an unbounded rate, causing integrity violations in historical opcode trace data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of requests each with gas=9999 (below GAS_SCALE_FACTOR), where scaleGas() returns 0 for each, so that gasLimitBucket is never consumed, allowing the attacker to saturate the historical EVM simulation pipeline and cause denial of service for legitimate Hashgraph history queries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on rateLimitBucket, which refills all tokens at once at the start of each second, to burst exactly requestsPerSecond requests at the boundary of each second window, effectively doubling the allowed throughput and flooding historical block queries that manipulate Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on gasLimitBucket to burst the full gasPerSecond capacity at the start of each second window, submitting a single request with gas equal to the full gasPerSecond limit to monopolize all historical EVM simulation capacity and starve other users from querying Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy on opcodeRateLimitBucket combined with the lack of SynchronizationStrategy to send concurrent opcode replay requests at second boundaries, bypassing the opcodeRequestsPerSecond=1 limit and replaying multiple historical Hashgraph transactions simultaneously, causing inconsistent historical state?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest where gas is set to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing scaleGas() to compute Math.floorDiv(Long.MAX_VALUE, 10000) which may overflow or produce an unexpectedly large token consumption value, crashing or permanently exhausting the gasLimitBucket and blocking all historical EVM simulations?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket uses Bucket.builder() without SynchronizationStrategy (defaulting to non-thread-safe local bucket) to send parallel requests that all pass tryConsume(1) simultaneously before any decrement is visible, bypassing the per-second request limit and flooding historical Hashgraph state queries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with gas=10001 (just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR), consuming only 1 scaled token from gasLimitBucket, while the actual EVM simulation uses the full 10001 gas against historical Hashgraph state, creating a systematic under-counting that allows far more historical simulation than the throttle intends?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the floor division in scaleGas() (Math.floorDiv(gas, 10000)) to systematically under-report gas consumption by up to 9999 gas units per request, accumulating a large discrepancy over many requests and allowing significantly more historical EVM simulation than the gasPerSecond limit intends?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket.addTokens() in restore() has no upper bound check (bucket4j addTokens can exceed capacity), by triggering many restore() calls through repeated InvalidParametersException paths, inflating the bucket beyond its configured capacity and allowing a burst of historical Hashgraph EVM simulations far exceeding the gasPerSecond limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send a sequence of requests where each request sets gas just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (e.g., 10001) to consume only 1 scaled token, but the actual EVM simulation against historical Hashgraph state consumes the full 10001 gas, allowing the attacker to run approximately 10000x more historical simulations than the gasPerSecond limit intends?
